_risk_level_doc: high — modifies shared infrastructure (score-update.sh + .gitignore + commands/close.md + commands/commit.md + commands/dev.md + agents/qa.md) that all downstream cycles depend on. Bounded blast radius: gate is a NEW pre-debate insertion in /close (Step 1.5) and /commit PRIMARY path; sandbox harnesses are NEW additive scripts; CAS+log-emission in score-update.sh is backward-compat-preserving (optional flag). Test-writer dispatch gate fires per commands/dev.md Step 8 (complexity_tier=STANDARD); acceptance_criteria_path below points to non-null AC JSON.

requirement.clarified: Implement all 3 TOP clusters (8 of 17 systemic issues) from docs/dev/meta-assessment-20260519-161035.json per user-requirement-dev-20260519-211515.md: (Cluster 1, AC1-AC5) shippability gate detecting gitignored / out-of-repo / ephemeral-mount artifacts in dev outputs; (Cluster 2, AC6-AC8) real-fixture sandboxed verification harness replacing --dry-run + PATH-shim mocks; (Cluster 3, AC9-AC13) event-sourced lifecycle log at logs/lifecycle.jsonl with CAS on score-update transitions plus orchestrator quota-wall + resumption events.
requirement.what: Create 6 new scripts (shippability-gate.py, sandbox-destructive.sh, sandbox-pressure.sh, sandbox-common.sh, lifecycle-log.sh, tmp-cleanup-install.sh) and modify 6 files (score-update.sh, close.md, commit.md, dev.md, qa.md, .gitignore) to land all 13 ACs.
requirement.why: Prior cycle 20260519-161035 (4-layer tmpfs prevention) shipped artifacts that were gitignored (docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md), out-of-repo (/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh), and on ephemeral mounts (/tmp/claude-pressure-warn-*); /close said YES; the deliverable was broken on origin/master. This cycle is the systemic backfill that prevents recurrence.
success_criteria: AC1: Gate fails closed on gitignored entry without waiver
AC2: Gate fails closed on out-of-repo path without system_file_waiver
AC3: Gate fails closed on ephemeral persistence without enospc_fallback_path
AC4: Gate applied to prior cycle 20260519-161035 dev-report (with persistent_state_files[] augmentation) fires all 3 reasons
AC5: Gate wiring is automatic in /close Step 1.5 — no manual orchestrator action
AC6: sandbox-destructive.sh exercises real script under mount-namespace remap with correct delete/preserve assertions
AC7: sandbox-pressure.sh manufactures real >75% mount and captures real hook output
AC8: agents/qa.md documents both harnesses + honors requires_fixture_verification flag
AC9: logs/lifecycle.jsonl receives valid JSONL with {ts, dev_session_id, event_type, agent, payload}
AC10: score-update.sh emits lifecycle entry on every score change with full payload
AC11: score-update.sh CAS race: concurrent calls — one succeeds, one fails with CAS_CONFLICT exit 3
AC12: Orchestrator quota-wall event writable via lifecycle-log.sh, documented in commands/dev.md
AC13: Orchestrator resumption event writable via lifecycle-log.sh, documented in commands/dev.md
constraints: All deliverables in-repo and not gitignored (gate dogfoods itself on this cycle's dev-report)
Existing score-update.sh callers MUST work unchanged (backward-compat binding)
Existing 4-layer tmpfs hooks (session-tmpfs-banner.sh, userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh, tmp-cleanup.sh) MUST NOT change behavior
Lifecycle log path: logs/lifecycle.jsonl (this repo's runtime path; user-text says .claude/logs/lifecycle.jsonl — multi-authority conflict resolved per ticket Reference Source section)
No new daemons / Prometheus / monitoring stacks (additive only)
unshare -m may be unavailable; harness must exit capability_unavailable=2 (NOT silent PASS)
Sandbox scratch must lie OUTSIDE git rev-parse --show-toplevel (codex C5: this repo IS .claude — repo-internal fallback would pollute on cleanup failure)

novelty_check.rationale: Prior cycle implemented runtime-prevention hooks at the system-runtime layer. This cycle implements meta-harness-prevention at the dev/QA harness layer (close-gate + commit-gate + sandbox-verification + lifecycle-audit). The two layers are orthogonal — this cycle's deliverables COULD have caught the prior cycle's R1/R2/R5a issues if they had existed in the prior cycle. NOT a re-attempt of tmpfs prevention; the systemic backfill that makes future cycles unable to ship the same class of broken artifacts.

setup.reason: non-UI -- agent-prompt edit + bash/python script creation + .gitignore edit + optional settings.json permissions; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps, API endpoints). All targets are dev/QA harness infrastructure.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: (R1) Every entry in dev.files_created / dev.files_modified is diffed against .gitignore. Gitignore match → flag unshippable_via_git; close gate critical-fail UNLESS dev-report has gitignore_waiver with rationale.
(R2) Any path matching system locations requires EITHER (a) an in-repo install manifest OR (b) system_file_waiver field
(R5a) Persistent-state file under ephemeral mounts must be flagged unless paired with an ENOSPC fallback path
Deliverable shape: a Python check script under scripts/, invoked during QA Step 11 OR during /close (pick ONE, document)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC5 (M3 picked /close; codex C1 added /commit PRIMARY belt-and-suspenders as M4)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: (R3) For destructive scheduled jobs: sandbox harness runs script non-dry-run
(R4) For threshold-conditional behaviors: pressure simulator creates real >threshold condition
Deliverable shape: reusable scripts under scripts/ plus invocation pattern documented in agents/qa.md
(W3) Quota-wall events record {ts, dev_session_id, agent_role, agent_id, tool_uses_at_cut, partial_artifacts}
(W5) Resumption events log {ts, dev_session_id, prior_agent_id, new_agent_id, recovery_notes}
(R9) Score-update transitions: replace direct score writes with append-only log entries; Reads use latest entry; writes use Compare-And-Swap on prev_score
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC9+AC10+AC11 (M9+M10+M11) with codex C7 reconciliation: scores.json authoritative for reads, log is audit-trail, CAS real under flock

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Deliverable shape: logging module writing to .claude/logs/lifecycle.jsonl (in-repo, NOT /tmp)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC9+AC10 (M9+M10+M11) with multi-authority conflict resolution: helper writes to logs/lifecycle.jsonl (the in-repo runtime path; user's .claude/logs mental path resolves to same file via /root/.claude symlink)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: All deliverables live at git-tracked paths (do NOT repeat the R1 gitignore mistake — none under docs/)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: implicit precondition verified by gate dogfooding itself on this cycle's dev-report

_acceptance_criteria_path_note: The file at this path currently contains stale 10-AC D+H content from a parallel cycle that shared the task-id. BA's tool-policy DOES NOT allow BA to overwrite docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-*.json (per policies/tool-policy.v1.json:117-136 — BA can write ticket-, context-, ba-spec-, ba-qa-report-, but NOT acceptance-criteria-). The 13 authoritative executable ACs for THIS cycle are embedded inline below in field `_inline_acceptance_criteria_13` AND fully described in ticket-20260519-211515.md AC1-AC13. The orchestrator MUST regenerate the canonical acceptance-criteria JSON file from those 13 ACs BEFORE dispatching test-writer (per commands/dev.md Step 8 + agents/test-writer.md Step 1). This is a downstream-pipeline gap surfaced by BA per the Analytical Authority refusal protocol.

_blast_radius_map_path_note: Like the AC JSON file above, the blast-radius-map.json under .claude/dev-registry/ is also outside BA's allowed write paths. The orchestrator should run scripts/blast-radius-tool.py against this cycle's files_to_modify list to populate the map BEFORE Dev dispatch (per spec-20260518-225715 §5.3). The map's expected coverage includes the 6 files_to_modify listed in development_approach.files_to_modify above.

development_approach.strategy: Cluster 1: scripts/shippability-gate.py reads dev-report JSON, runs 3 checks (gitignore via git check-ignore --no-index, out-of-repo via realpath + git rev-parse, ephemeral-mount via path-prefix + persistent_state_files[]), writes verdict JSON to docs/dev/shippability-gate-<task-id>.json, exit 0/1/2. Wired into commands/close.md as new Step 1.5 between inspector dispatch and QA debate. Belt-and-suspenders at commands/commit.md PRIMARY (non-force) path. Cluster 2: scripts/sandbox-{destructive,pressure}.sh use unshare -m + bind-mounts to remap /tmp /dev/shm /var/log /var/tmp; scratch at /var/lib/claude-sandbox; capability_unavailable=2 on missing unshare -m. Shared scripts/sandbox-common.sh enforces no-scratch-in-repo-root. agents/qa.md Step 11 documents the harnesses + honors requires_fixture_verification BA-spec field. Cluster 3: scripts/lifecycle-log.sh appends JSONL to logs/lifecycle.jsonl. scripts/score-update.sh adds optional --expected-prev-score, performs CAS under flock, invokes lifecycle-log.sh on success. .gitignore adds !/logs/ + !/logs/lifecycle.jsonl narrow exceptions. commands/dev.md adds documented call-site templates for AC12 quota-wall + AC13 resumption events.
development_approach.validation_approach: Self-dogfood: dev MUST run python3 scripts/shippability-gate.py --dev-report docs/dev/dev-report-20260519-211515.json BEFORE handing off to QA — all 13 files must pass. AC1-AC5 via gate fixture files. AC6-AC7 via sandbox-* invocations on real prior-cycle scripts (or capability_unavailable=2 if unshare -m missing). AC8 via grep of agents/qa.md. AC9 via single lifecycle-log.sh invocation + jq parse. AC10 via score-update.sh invocation + grep lifecycle.jsonl. AC11 via two-parallel-call test fixture with same --expected-prev-score. AC12+AC13 via lifecycle-log.sh synthetic-event invocations + commands/dev.md grep for call-site templates.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) reviewed 3 architecture decisions + 5 explicit questions. Returned 13 findings on Decision 1 (gate invocation), Decision 2 (sandbox shape), Decision 3a-3e (lifecycle log details), plus 4 missed-pitfall items. All 13 were classified in_scope_real_bug (12) or in_scope_minor (1). All applied. Key changes triggered by codex: (a) gate also lands in /commit PRIMARY path as belt-and-suspenders, not just /close (C1); (b) sandbox uses unshare -m + bind-mounts not just scratch dir, capability_unavailable on missing namespace (C3+C4); (c) sandbox refuses repo-internal fallback because this repo IS .claude (C5); (d) --expected-prev-score is optional not required for backward-compat (C6); (e) scores.json remains authoritative for reads, log is audit-trail (C7); (f) .gitignore exception scoped to /logs/lifecycle.jsonl specifically not broad logs/ (C8); (g) AC12/AC13 require minimal orchestrator call-site wiring in commands/dev.md not just helper-demo (C9); (h) git check-ignore --no-index flag required (C10); (i) realpath canonicalization needed for symlinked /root/.claude paths (C11); (j) persistent_state_files[] field added to dev-report contract per codex C12 (prior cycle's /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-* was only in script rationale, not files_created).

findings.issue: C1: /close --force short-circuits before inspectors; /commit --force/--bulk also bypass close-gate. 'Every /close passes it' is false.
findings.proposed_fix: Keep Option B (/close) as required normal-path gate; document 'normal /close only; human force/bulk paths are audited overrides'; add belt-and-suspenders at /commit PRIMARY non-force path.
findings.rationale: Closes the bypass for normal-path commits while documenting that force/bulk remain audited overrides. M4 added to spec; documented in Out-of-Scope Observations.

findings.issue: C2: Document scope limit explicitly (normal /close only; force/bulk are audited overrides)
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit doc in spec + commit.md + close.md notes
findings.rationale: Risk 6 in ticket Edge Cases section.

findings.issue: C3: scratch dir alone doesn't exercise real scripts hardcoded to /tmp /dev/shm /var/log — sandbox design incomplete
findings.proposed_fix: Use unshare -m mount namespace + bind-mounts so unmodified scripts see sandboxed /tmp /var/tmp /dev/shm /var/log
findings.rationale: Without mount-namespace, sandbox-destructive can't exercise /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh which hardcodes /tmp. M5+M6 updated to use unshare -m + bind-mounts.

findings.issue: C4: If namespace unavailable, do NOT report PASS — fail with capability_unavailable
findings.proposed_fix: Capability check at sandbox-common.sh entry; exit 2 (capability_unavailable) if unshare -m -- true fails. NOT silent PASS.

findings.issue: C5: avoid $HOME/.claude/sandbox fallback — this repo IS .claude; failed cleanup pollutes repo
findings.proposed_fix: sandbox-common.sh refuses any scratch path inside git rev-parse --show-toplevel. Primary: /var/lib/claude-sandbox/; if mkdir fails → exit capability_unavailable=2. No repo-internal fallback.

findings.issue: C6: --expected-prev-score required violates 'callers continue unchanged' backward-compat
findings.proposed_fix: Make --expected-prev-score optional; if supplied compare inside flock; if omitted, read implicit expected inside flock then compare. Mismatch exits 3 CAS_CONFLICT.
findings.rationale: M10 updated. Preserves old CLI; AC11 still verifiable via test fixture passing same --expected-prev-score to both callers.

findings.issue: C7: If lifecycle JSONL is source of truth, score-inject.sh / projection must read latest log entry. Treating agent-scores.json as authoritative does not satisfy 'reads use latest entry'.
findings.proposed_fix: Either (a) build projection script that reads latest log entry, OR (b) document reconciliation: scores.json remains authoritative-for-reads; log is audit-trail-only.
findings.rationale: Adopted (b) — scores.json authoritative, log is audit. AC9/AC10/AC11 all satisfiable under (b). Logged in Out-of-Scope Observations for future (a) cycle.

findings.issue: C8: gitignore path — actual runtime path is logs/lifecycle.jsonl (repo root IS .claude). Add narrow exceptions !/logs/ + !/logs/lifecycle.jsonl, NOT broad !.claude/logs/.
findings.proposed_fix: Add narrow exception scoped to literal file at repo root; don't unignore the whole logs/ tree.
findings.rationale: M11 updated. Multi-authority conflict (user said .claude/logs/lifecycle.jsonl but repo runtime is logs/lifecycle.jsonl) resolved per ticket Reference Source section.

findings.issue: C9: AC12/AC13 — helper-only demo is not enough. The ACs say orchestrator writes events when lifecycle situations occur. Wire minimal call sites.
findings.proposed_fix: Add documented call-site templates in commands/dev.md resumption + quota-wall sections; full detection logic deferred to followup
findings.rationale: M12 added. AC text says 'when synthetic event' — call-site template + synthetic test satisfies.

findings.issue: C10: Use git check-ignore -v --no-index so tracked-but-ignored paths are still detected
findings.proposed_fix: Pass --no-index flag in gate's git check-ignore invocation

findings.issue: C11: Normalize symlinked repo paths (/root/.claude/... resolves inside /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude); don't false-fail those as out-of-repo
findings.proposed_fix: Canonicalize via realpath before comparing to git rev-parse --show-toplevel
findings.rationale: M1 system-path check uses realpath; Risk 2 in ticket.

findings.issue: C12: R5a detection should not rely only on dev.files_created/modified — prior cycle's /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-* was in rationale, not as created file. Require persistent_state_files[] or scan declared runtime-state paths.
findings.proposed_fix: Add persistent_state_files[] required field to dev-report contract; additionally grep shipped script sources for hardcoded ephemeral paths (warning, not hard-fail)
findings.rationale: M1 ephemeral-mount check now reads persistent_state_files[]; Risk 3 in ticket. New dev-report contract field — dev must populate.

findings.issue: C13: Add permissions/settings for new scripts if they must run without manual prompts
findings.proposed_fix: Document in Technical Hints; dev to add allowlist entries to settings.json if needed

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 13 codex findings applied. 12 in_scope_real_bug + 1 in_scope_minor. 0 OBSERVATION_ONLY, 0 rejected, 0 nitpicks. Specifically: M4 added (commit.md belt-and-suspenders); M5+M6 use unshare -m; M7 enforces no-repo-scratch; M10 --expected-prev-score optional; M11 narrow gitignore exception; M12 commands/dev.md call-site templates; M1 uses --no-index + realpath + persistent_state_files[] field.

specialists_assessed.ui-specialist: SKIP — pure harness infrastructure, no UI
specialists_assessed.architect: SKIP — codex enforced provides adversarial architectural review at every BA/dev/QA stage
